footer.description: Bringing the art of tea ceremony to Wellington. Experience tranquility, mindfulness, and the beauty of tea culture.

hero.title: Experience the Art of Tea
hero.description: Discover the traditions of tea ceremony in Wellington, where every cup tells a story of mindfulness and connection.

intro.title: Why Moso Tea
intro.description: We bring the art of tea ceremony to New Zealand, creating moments of peace and connection in our carefully curated space.
intro.feature1Description: Rooted in centuries of tradition, we bring the art and philosophy of tea ceremony to life — one mindful cup at a time.
intro.feature2Description: Nestled in the hills of Wainuiomata, our workshop is a place where time slows down and the outside world quietly falls away.
intro.feature3Description: From the first scent of leaves to the last sip, every session is crafted to be unhurried, and deeply personal.

experiences.title: Our Workshops
experiences.subtitle: Two unique experiences — discover the art of tea ceremony or craft your own tea from leaf to cup.

workshopA.title: Tea Ceremony Experience
workshopA.description: A guided 90-minute journey through tea culture — from our garden to the tea table. Learn the story of tea, explore our tea plants, and experience an authentic Taiwanese-style tea ceremony.

workshopB.title: Tea Making Experience
workshopB.description: A hands-on seasonal workshop where you pick fresh tea leaves from our garden and learn to process them into your own finished tea to take home.

testimonials.title: Guest Reflections
testimonials.quote1: A truly transformative experience. The attention to detail and the peaceful atmosphere made me feel like I was transported to another world.
testimonials.quote2: The perfect escape from city life. I've attended three sessions now and each one brings new insights and a sense of calm.
testimonials.quote3: An authentic and beautifully curated tea ceremony. The tea master's knowledge and gentle guidance made it accessible for beginners like me.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: A glimpse into the world of Moso Tea — our space, our craft, and the moments we share.

hero.title: Our Story
hero.subtitle: Sharing the art of tea since 2021

story.title: A Journey of Tea
story.p1: Moso Tea was born from a simple yet profound love for the art of tea ceremony. Founded in 2021, our workshop has become a sanctuary for those seeking moments of stillness in Wainuiomata, Wellington.
story.p2: The name "Moso" carries two quiet meanings. In Taiwanese dialect, moso means slow — an invitation to pause, to breathe, and to be present. It is also the name of the moso bamboo, a plant we deeply admire not for its dramatic growth, but for its stillness and resilience. The bamboo stands quietly through all seasons and rooted and unhurried. Our tea trees came to us by chance, much like the bamboo — an unexpected gift that found its way into our lives and shaped everything that followed.
story.p3: What began as a small gathering of tea enthusiasts has grown into a beloved space where people from all walks of life come together to experience the meditative beauty of tea culture.
story.p4: Our lifestyle block workshop in Wainuiomata offers a rare opportunity to step away from the pace of everyday life and immerse yourself in a tradition — one that celebrates the simple, unhurried act of preparing and sharing tea.

philosophy.quote: In the simple act of making tea, we find a path to presence. Each cup is an invitation to slow down, to breathe, and to connect with the moment.
philosophy.quoteZh: 在泡茶的簡單動作中，我們找到了通往當下的道路。每一杯茶都是一份邀請，讓我們放慢腳步，深呼吸，與當下連結。

expect.title: What to Expect
expect.item1Description: Our experienced tea masters guide you through a deep cultural knowledge to every session.
expect.item2Description: A quiet, peaceful space where you can slow down, focus on the moment, and enjoy a state of calm and balanced tea experience.
expect.item3Description: Premium teas and traditional utensils are included in every experience.
expect.item4Description: Our sessions are designed to give you time to fully absorb and appreciate each moment.

founder1.name: Jenson
founder1.description: Jenson is the heart of every ceremony at Moso Tea. Originally from Taiwan, she brings a lifetime of tea culture into each session — his warmth and gentle guidance make every guest feel at home from the very first cup.

founder2.name: Joyce
founder2.description: Joyce tends to the tea garden and oversees the growing of every leaf. Her quiet dedication to the land and the craft is what gives Moso Tea its roots — quite literally.

founder3.name: Lynn
founder3.description: Lynn grew up surrounded by tea. Now she shares that world with guests in her own words, bridging cultures and making the art of tea feel accessible, engaging, and alive.

cta.title: Ready to Experience Tea?
cta.description: Join us for an unforgettable journey into the art of tea ceremony. Book your experience today and discover the tranquility that awaits.

hero.title: Our Workshops
hero.description: Two unique experiences to discover the world of tea — choose the journey that speaks to you.

workshopA.label: Workshop A
workshopA.title: Tea Ceremony Experience
workshopA.subtitle: 90 minutes  ·  Max 8 guests  ·  NZD $75 per person
workshopA.description: A guided journey through tea culture — from our garden to the tea table. Experience an authentic Taiwanese-style tea ceremony in our peaceful space.

items.2: Tea garden tour — explore our tea plants and learn about Camellia sinensis varieties
items.3: Discover the story of tea: from ancient China to modern tea culture
items.4: Demo the Taiwanese tea ceremony — brewing team, serving tea
items.5: Learn to appreciate tea: observe the colour, smell the aroma, taste the flavour

workshopB.label: Workshop B
workshopB.title: Tea Making Experience
workshopB.subtitle: 2 hours  ·  NZD $85 / person  ·  Seasonal
workshopB.description: A hands-on seasonal workshop where you pick fresh tea leaves from our garden and learn to process them into your own finished tea. Due to the seasonal nature of tea growth, this experience is only available during harvest periods.

items.1: Garden walk and introduction to tea plant varieties
items.2: Hand-pick fresh tea leaves from our garden
items.3: Learn traditional tea processing — withering, rolling, and oxidation
items.overnightNote: The following steps require overnight processing and cannot be completed during the session. Your host will explain the process.
items.4: Fire and dry the tea leaves using traditional techniques
items.5: Brew and taste finished tea from our garden (we prepare a completed batch for you to taste)
items.6: Receive your handmade tea by post once fully processed (NZ domestic only)

workshopB.seasonalNote: This experience depends on tea leaf availability. Register your interest and we will contact you when sessions are available.

faq.title: Common Questions
faq.q1: Do I need any prior experience with tea ceremonies?
faq.a1: Not at all! Our experiences are designed for all levels, from complete beginners to seasoned tea enthusiasts. Your host will guide you through every step, ensuring you feel comfortable and engaged throughout the session.
faq.a2: We recommend comfortable, modest clothing in muted colours. Please remove your shoes upon entering the tea room. Since our experiences include a walk through the tea garden, we also recommend wearing comfortable closed-toe shoes suitable for light outdoor walking.
faq.a5: We offer full refunds for cancellations made at least 24 hours before your scheduled session. Cancellations within 24 hours may be rescheduled subject to availability. Please contact us if you have any concerns about your booking.
faq.q6: Do I need to avoid wearing perfume or cologne?
faq.a6: Yes, we kindly ask all guests to refrain from wearing perfume, cologne, or strongly scented products. The delicate aromas of tea are central to the experience, and strong fragrances can interfere with everyone's enjoyment.
faq.q7: Will there be insects in the garden?
faq.a7: As our tea plants are grown in a natural outdoor environment, you may occasionally see small insects, just like in any garden.
